topics.label: Agent Skills
topics.description: Creating, distributing, and troubleshooting Claude Code skills: SKILL.md frontmatter, semantic triggers, allowed-tools, progressive disclosure, subagents, and hooks.

examScenarios.title: Skill Activation Failure
examScenarios.prompt: A skill never activates when the user types /my-skill. Walk through the diagnostic steps.
examScenarios.focus: Skill directory path configuration and frontmatter validation

examScenarios.title: Enterprise Skill Rollout
examScenarios.prompt: Your organization wants all engineers to have access to a code-review skill without manual installation. How do you distribute it?
examScenarios.focus: Enterprise-managed settings and skill distribution mechanisms

topics.label: Claude API
topics.description: Building with the Anthropic messages API: endpoints, streaming via SSE, tool use blocks, few-shot prompting, prompt caching, extended thinking, RAG, batch API, and agentic parallelization.

examScenarios.title: Cost Optimization for Batch Workload
examScenarios.prompt: You need to classify 50,000 support tickets overnight. Which API feature reduces cost, and how do you structure the requests?
examScenarios.focus: Batch API usage and cost trade-offs vs streaming

examScenarios.title: Multi-Turn Tool Use
examScenarios.prompt: Claude returns a tool_use block. Describe the exact follow-up request structure required to continue the conversation.
examScenarios.focus: tool_use and tool_result block flow

topics.label: Model Context Protocol
topics.description: MCP fundamentals: the three primitives (tools, resources, prompts), Python SDK decorators, stdio transport, templated resources, the MCP Server Inspector, and client-server request-response flow.

examScenarios.title: Primitive Selection
examScenarios.prompt: You are building an MCP server that exposes a company wiki search, a Slack message sender, and a pre-configured incident report template. Map each to the correct primitive and justify your choices.
examScenarios.focus: Correct assignment of tools, resources, and prompts

examScenarios.title: Debugging a New MCP Server
examScenarios.prompt: Your MCP server starts without errors but the tool is not visible to the client. How do you diagnose the issue?
examScenarios.focus: MCP Server Inspector usage and JSON-RPC troubleshooting

topics.label: Claude Code
topics.description: Using Claude Code as an agentic CLI: CLAUDE.md scoping, Plan Mode, custom /commands, the Bash tool, subagents with tool restrictions, MCP integration, GitHub PR automation, /compact for context management, and extended thinking.

examScenarios.title: Context Window Management
examScenarios.prompt: Midway through a long refactoring session, Claude Code warns that the context window is nearly full. What command do you run and what does it do?
examScenarios.focus: /compact command and context management strategy

examScenarios.title: Safe Delegation with Subagents
examScenarios.prompt: You want Claude to write documentation for a library without any risk of it modifying source files. How do you configure this safely?
examScenarios.focus: Subagent tool restrictions and allowed-tools configuration

topics.label: Claude Cowork
topics.description: Using Claude Cowork for autonomous background tasks: task loop mechanics, isolated VM execution, scheduled task cadences and sleep/wake behavior, plugins, skills and connectors, web fetch limitations, local file permission modes, research workflows, sub-agents for parallel workstreams, memory scope, and interactive steering.

examScenarios.title: Missed Scheduled Run
examScenarios.prompt: A daily Cowork task was scheduled for 9 AM but the desktop app was closed. The user opens the app at 2 PM. What happens?
examScenarios.focus: Scheduled task sleep/wake behavior and skipped run history

examScenarios.title: Enterprise Plugin Distribution
examScenarios.prompt: Your team needs all engineers to have a shared Cowork connector to the internal ticketing system. How do you distribute it?
examScenarios.focus: Plugin bundling and company-level customization

topics.label: Claude Code 101
topics.description: Foundational Claude Code concepts: agentic tools vs chat, the agentic loop, Explore-Plan-Code-Commit workflow, context commands (/compact, /clear), CLAUDE.md for persistent memory, permission modes, MCP server integration, hooks, and custom subagents.

examScenarios.title: Selective Tool Blocking
examScenarios.prompt: A team wants auto-accept for file operations but full human review for every shell command. How do they implement this without disabling auto-accept globally?
examScenarios.focus: Combining auto-accept mode with PreToolUse hooks to block specific tools

examScenarios.title: Session Continuity vs Clean Slate
examScenarios.prompt: A developer is deep into a refactoring session. The context is nearly full but switching topics entirely would be wasteful. What is the right command and why?
examScenarios.focus: /compact vs /clear trade-offs and context management strategy

topics.label: MCP Advanced
topics.description: Advanced MCP concepts: sampling (server-initiated LLM calls), progress and logging notifications via context objects, roots-based security boundaries, JSON-RPC request vs notification architecture, stdio initialization handshake, Streamable HTTP with SSE, transport selection, and production scaling patterns.

examScenarios.title: Transport Selection for Deployment
examScenarios.prompt: You have three deployment scenarios: (1) a subprocess on the same machine with no network access, (2) a cloud service behind a load balancer, (3) a legacy client that only supports persistent SSE connections. Which transport fits each case?
examScenarios.focus: stdio vs Streamable HTTP vs HTTP+SSE trade-offs

examScenarios.title: Roots Boundary Enforcement
examScenarios.prompt: A client declares roots: [file:///project/src]. Your server tool receives a path traversal attempt targeting /project/.env. What is the correct response and why?
examScenarios.focus: Roots security boundary enforcement and path validation

topics.label: Subagents
topics.description: Claude Code subagent patterns: isolated context windows, input and summary-return mechanics, defining agents in .claude/agents/, structured output requirements, tool access restrictions, delegation value vs anti-patterns, parallel orchestration trade-offs, and building task-specific assistants.

examScenarios.title: Read-Only Code Reviewer
examScenarios.prompt: Build a reusable code-reviewer subagent that reads any given file, applies a checklist, and returns a Markdown report. Specify the definition location, tool set, and output format.
examScenarios.focus: .claude/agents/ definition, allowed-tools whitelist, structured output design

examScenarios.title: Parallel Module Analysis
examScenarios.prompt: You want to analyze five independent modules simultaneously. How do you orchestrate this with subagents, and what coordination challenges must you plan for?
examScenarios.focus: Parallel delegation, result aggregation, and write-conflict prevention

topics.label: Agentic Orchestration
topics.description: Building and controlling agentic loops on the Claude API: stop_reason-based loop control, the Task tool for subagent spawning, parallel delegation patterns, session management with --resume and fork_session, and deterministic enforcement with PreToolUse hooks.

studyPointers.label: Claude API — Agentic Loop and stop_reason
Claude Code — Task Tool and Subagent Orchestration
Claude Code — Hooks and Deterministic Enforcement

examScenarios.title: Loop Termination Bug
examScenarios.prompt: An agentic loop never stops even after Claude finishes its work. Diagnose the most likely cause and describe the correct fix.
examScenarios.focus: stop_reason-based termination vs text parsing anti-pattern

examScenarios.title: Financial Threshold Enforcement
examScenarios.prompt: A system prompt rule fails to prevent Claude from approving expenses above a threshold. What is the root cause and how do you enforce the threshold reliably?
examScenarios.focus: Probabilistic prompts vs deterministic PreToolUse hooks

topics.label: Context Reliability
topics.description: Patterns for reliable multi-turn and multi-agent pipelines: persistent case facts across compaction, lost-in-the-middle mitigation, escalation triggers, structured error propagation, conflicting source handling, aggregate vs stratified evaluation, coordinator decomposition debugging, and the batch API vs sync API trade-off.

studyPointers.label: Claude Code — Context Compaction and Persistent State
Claude API — Context Ordering and Lost-in-the-Middle
Claude API — Evaluation Strategy and Stratified Sampling

examScenarios.title: Context Compaction Data Loss
examScenarios.prompt: A support agent loses claim numbers after compaction. Describe the architectural pattern that prevents transactional data loss across context windows.
examScenarios.focus: Persistent case facts block injected outside summarized history

examScenarios.title: Aggregate Accuracy Trap
examScenarios.prompt: A team reduces human review after reaching 94% accuracy but later discovers a critical document type has 61% accuracy. What evaluation practice would have caught this before the reduction?
examScenarios.focus: Stratified sampling and per-segment analysis
